title: TRAKINFO support
author: cyon-e
body:
### Game

All

### Problem Description

The 2024 Release added a TRAKINFO Lump to select between 3 Soundtracks.
The usual midi, the the recorded Roland MIDI and Andrew Hulshults IDKFA Remix


### Feature Description

It allows the user to switch music tracks defined in the TRAKINFO Lump

### Additional Media

usefull comments from https://github.com/fabiangreffrath/woof/pull/1982 :
---
> So, TRAKINFO is a simple assignment from SHA1 of the original MIDI lump to a new music remix lump name?

Yes, it looks like this:
```
	//D_INTRO
	"0c0acce45130bab935d2f1e85664b29a3c724fcd" : 
	{
		"MIDI" : "O_INTRO",
		"Remixed" : "H_INTRO"
	},
```
`O_*` files are Roland SC-55 recodings, `H_*` files are excellent Andrew Hulshult [remix](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B4ytPQuLGzs).

> Is there a spec somewhere?

No. TRAKINFO has comments and in a different format compared to the ID24 spec.

---

> @ceski-1 Remix from KEX port is very loud compared to MIDI music. Should we do something about it?

Hi, sorry to barge in, just throwing my two cents in. Since Update 1 dropped for KEX Doom a `volume` property that does exactly what it implies, and was used to increase the volume of the relatively quiet SC55 recordings of the OST. They can be seen on the second `TRAKINFO` lump in the KEX `extras.wad`

```JSONC
// O_E1M1
"b292d36331c81e1f2ee4d7bd2938e7fedb39f172" : 
{
	"volume" : "2.0"
},
```

title: Flat bleed isn't taking into consideration display height differences due to special 242
author: SirBofu
body: ### UZDoom Version UZDoom 4.14.3 ### Game Doom ### Operating System Windows 11 ### OS Version _No response_ ### Hardware _No response_ ### Duplicate Confirmation - [x] I have searched and not found a similar issue. ### Bug Description There is a display issue **(hardware renderer only)** when a map relies upon a sector bleed effect on a sector whose floor is being raised by Boom linedef special 242. This can be seen in MAP55 of [the latest Dominus Diabolicus build](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/mcuuqttc86f1zodegdkk4/DominusD2-July9.zip?rlkey=slwc17fy5bscvmgmlz9fg9dv1&st=pvdv9eti&dl=0) in 1292, -440: The main nukage pit, sectors 14 and 391, has a 242 special setting its height from -32 to -24. Sectors 84 and 389 are set at height -152 within the nukage pit with a different floor texture and are intended to rise up out of the nukage when the player crosses a certain linedef via a trigger. In DSDA-Doom and other ports, the bleed effect from the surrounding sectors, due to the absence of the expected back sidedef, causes nukage to be drawn at the height of -32 for these sectors until they rise out of the nukage; however, in UZDoom, the nukage is drawn at height -32, with the void drawn where the indentation occurs. ### Replication 1. Downlooad the attached .WAD (Doom2, MAP01): [UZDoom242Bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29864220/UZDoom242Bug.zip) 2. Start a new game loading the wad in another port, such as DSDA-Doom or Woof! 3. Observe that in the pool, the surface of the pool is constant until the switch is raised and a pillar rises out of it. 4. Load the WAD in UZDoom. 5. Observe that in the spot where the pillar is, there is a black hole, and a slightly smaller patch of water can be seen at the edges of said hole. ### Configuration ```ini ``` ### Log _No response_

title: Add an option to enabled the old-school RED RED pain flash for Doom games
author: Lemono-o
body: ### Game Doom ### Problem Description Bring back the blinding redness! please :) ### Feature Description Y'know when you eat a rocket to the face you get a beautiful shade of painful red for like 5ish solid seconds? I miss that blinding red screen when taking mega damage, it really sold the pain. But in more modern ports like UzDoom here the redness is capped, so you can actually SEE. That's sacrilegious if you ask me, bring back the legacy red screen effect! I'm sure I won't regret asking for this later.. The blinding red screen effect is too funny and panic-educing to be forgotten to time! The default UzDoom effect should probably be kept as the default, blood flashbangs are probably an acquired taste TL:DR, Add an option to make the red pain effect more true to Classic Doom, even when at the cost of visibility. See Chocolate Doom for examples. Oh and let it work with hardware rendering pls. Thanks! Oh and I already tried editing the .ini file to crank up the existing "Blood_Flade_Scalar" does not produce the desired effect, rip ### Additional Media UzDoom Sacralige, odd amount of sight after eating an explosion Classic holyness, can't see shit

This does not happen on OpenGL and the issue seems to resolve itself when entering a map. The ENDOOM screen is also green when the game is quit before entering a map. ### Replication 1. Launch the game using the Vulkan renderer. 2. Do not enter the maps. ### Configuration ```ini ``` ### Log _No response_

title: Sprites on the automap are four times the size if you double the Scale Factor or use Downsampling.
author: TheFerries
body: ### UZDoom Version UZDoom 4.14.3 ### Game All ### Operating System Windows 10 ### OS Version Windows 10 Pro ### Hardware GTX 1050ti ### Duplicate Confirmation - [x] I have searched and not found a similar issue. ### Bug Description What the title says, if you set the scale factor to 2.0 or downsample a higher resolution to your monitor (mine is 4k to a 1080p so it works as a nice 4x supersampling option). The sprites on the automap appear quite big compared to the usual. As an example, here's map12 from Community Chest 3: -Here's how it looks without any of these 2 options: The map itself is rather big, so its already pretty zoomed out by default, but the sprites look around the correct size. -Here's how it looks with either setting the scale factor to 2.0 or by downsampling 4k to 1080p: They sure grow in size, they appear way too big and sure makes it feel like the map looks smaller than it actually is, not to mention it makes it harder to see stuff like ammo boxes or secret areas, not to mention groups of enemies look like they're clipping each other. Dunno if this is intended behavour, since its 4 times the pixels, but I wonder if it could look like it does in the first image. ### Replication If you have a 1080p monitor, just set the scale factor to 2.0 to see it, or just set the resolution preset to 4k. ### Configuration Don't think its necessary here. ### Log It's not a crash or game error, so I also don't think it's necessary.

title: Serialization of zero values in ACS-arrays for GDCC-compiled mods in save files
author: coold
body:
### UZDoom Version

UZDoom 4.14.3 trunk

### Game

Doom

### Operating System

Windows 11

### OS Version

Windows 11

### Hardware

7800X3D, 4900rtx, 16GB

### Duplicate Confirmation

- [x] I have searched and not found a similar issue.

### Bug Description

After playing for some time in DoomRPG my quicksave became to so quick. The longer you play - the longer save will freeze the game. Also unpacked save file can be hundreds of megs.
It can be mostly caused by DoomRPG, but also UZDoom itself serialize zero values from ACS arrays as is. 
But as I understood zero values unreachable anyway, so it can cause save file bloat, and thus increase save time and freeze.
My naive version of this fix https://github.com/UZDoom/UZDoom/pull/1571

### Replication

I don`t know any other way except just to play DoomRPG for a time longer than 30 minutes

### Configuration

```ini

```

### Log

_No response_

title: Segfault when parsing LANGUAGE.csv in UZArchipelago-0.8.5.pk3
author: 0dot0repeating
body:
### UZDoom Version

from 5.0.0-pre-93-1947b9d8ef to current commit (9a78750ffd)

### Game

All

### Operating System

Linux x86_64

### OS Version

Arch Linux x86_64, kernel 7.0.7-arch2-1

### Hardware

CPU: AMD 7700x, GPU: AMD 6750XT

### Duplicate Confirmation

- [x] I have searched and not found a similar issue.

### Bug Description

UZDoom segfaults on startup while parsing lines 7, 34, 35, 37-43, and 138 of [the LANGUAGE.csv](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29735627/LANGUAGE.csv) in [UZArchipelago-0.8.5.pk3](https://github.com/ToxicFrog/doom-mods/releases/tag/gzap-0.8.5), all of which are missing the Remarks column. Adding a comma to the end of each of these lines (aka. adding the column) resolves the segfault.

This was first noticed in a build made sometime in June (forgot the commit, sorry), was reconfirmed in the current trunk commit (9a78750ffd), and through git-bisect I've determined that it first appears in commit 1947b9d8ef.

### Replication

1. `uzdoom -config /dev/null -iwad path/to/doom2.wad -file path/to/UZArchipelago-0.8.5.pk3`
2.  Segfault :(

### Configuration

```ini
Completely fresh config
```

### Log

[stdout.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29735514/stdout.log)
[uzdoom-crash.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29735457/uzdoom-crash.log)

title: Cross-compilation is broken
author: ValdikSS
body:
### UZDoom Version

git master

### Game

All

### Operating System

Linux x86_64

### OS Version

_No response_

### Hardware

_No response_

### Duplicate Confirmation

- [x] I have searched and not found a similar issue.

### Bug Description

I was trying to compile UZDoom for ARM64 on x86_64 machine using regular Debian/Ubuntu cross-compilation architecture. Debian/Ubuntu in dpkg-buildpackage has a special set of wrappers which automate cross-compilation for many build systems, including CMake.

Ultimately, that did not work without commenting everything related to CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING.
It seems that CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING was added as a special case of cross-compiling for OS X PowerPC ["Cross compiling without Rosetta"](https://zdoom.org/w/index.php?title=Compile_ZDoom_on_Mac_OS_X#Configuring_for_cross_compiling:~:text=Cross%20compiling%20without%20Rosetta)

For anything else, it prevents cross-compiling as far as I understand.

I'm not an expert in CMake that's why not making a PR, but that's what worked for me. `ZCMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING` is used as a commenting out measure.

```diff
diff --git a/CMakeLists.txt b/CMakeLists.txt
index de68be2..2873682 100644
--- a/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -60,7 +60,7 @@ if( FORCE_CROSSCOMPILE )
 	set( CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING TRUE )
 endif()
 
-if(CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING)
+if(ZCMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING)
 	set(IMPORT_EXECUTABLES "IMPORTFILE-NOTFOUND" CACHE FILEPATH "Export file from native build.")
 	include(${IMPORT_EXECUTABLES})
 endif()
@@ -99,7 +99,7 @@ function( add_pk3 PK3_NAME )
 	string( REPLACE "." "_" PK3_TARGET ${PK3_NAME} )
 
 	add_custom_command( OUTPUT ${ZDOOM_OUTPUT_DIR}/${PK3_NAME}
-		COMMAND zipdir -udf ${PK3_ZIPDIR_OPTIONS} ${ZDOOM_OUTPUT_DIR}/${PK3_NAME} ${ARGN}
+		COMMAND ${ZDOOM_OUTPUT_DIR}/tools/zipdir/zipdir -udf ${PK3_ZIPDIR_OPTIONS} ${ZDOOM_OUTPUT_DIR}/${PK3_NAME} ${ARGN}
 		DEPENDS zipdir )
 
 	# Create a list of source files for this PK3, for use in the IDE
@@ -459,7 +459,7 @@ set( ZMUSIC_LIBRARIES zmusic )
 
 set( LZMA_INCLUDE_DIR "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/libraries/lzma/C" )
 
-if( NOT CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
+if( NOT ZCMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
 	if( NOT CROSS_EXPORTS )
 		set( CROSS_EXPORTS "" )
 	endif()
@@ -493,6 +493,6 @@ if( ZMUSIC_SYSTEM_INSTALL )
 	add_subdirectory( libraries/miniz )
 endif()
 
-if( NOT CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
+if( NOT ZCMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
 	export(TARGETS ${CROSS_EXPORTS} FILE "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/ImportExecutables.cmake" )
 endif()
diff --git a/libraries/ZVulkan/src/glslang/glslang/OSDependent/Unix/CMakeLists.txt b/libraries/ZVulkan/src/glslang/glslang/OSDependent/Unix/CMakeLists.txt
index 354a3e9..4b133d1 100644
--- a/libraries/ZVulkan/src/glslang/glslang/OSDependent/Unix/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/libraries/ZVulkan/src/glslang/glslang/OSDependent/Unix/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -37,7 +37,7 @@ set_property(TARGET OSDependent PROPERTY POSITION_INDEPENDENT_CODE ON)
 
 # Link pthread
 set(CMAKE_THREAD_PREFER_PTHREAD ON)
-if(${CMAKE_VERSION} VERSION_LESS "3.1.0" OR CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING)
+if(${CMAKE_VERSION} VERSION_LESS "3.1.0" OR ZCMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING)
     # Needed as long as we support CMake 2.8 for Ubuntu 14.04,
     # which does not support the recommended Threads::Threads target.
     # https://cmake.org/cmake/help/v2.8.12/cmake.html#module:FindThreads
diff --git a/src/CMakeLists.txt b/src/CMakeLists.txt
index dc4d936..226a510 100644
--- a/src/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/src/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -495,15 +495,15 @@ else()
 endif()
 
 add_custom_command( OUTPUT ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/xlat_parser.c ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/xlat_parser.h
-	COMMAND lemon -C${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR} ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/gamedata/xlat/xlat_parser.y
+	COMMAND ${ZDOOM_OUTPUT_DIR}/tools/lemon/lemon -C${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR} ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/gamedata/xlat/xlat_parser.y
 	DEPENDS lemon ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/gamedata/xlat/xlat_parser.y )
 
 add_custom_command( OUTPUT ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/zcc-parse.c ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/zcc-parse.h
-	COMMAND lemon -C${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR} ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/scripting/frontend/zcc-parse.lemon
+	COMMAND ${ZDOOM_OUTPUT_DIR}/tools/lemon/lemon -C${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR} ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/scripting/frontend/zcc-parse.lemon
 	DEPENDS lemon ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/scripting/frontend/zcc-parse.lemon )
 
 add_custom_command( OUTPUT ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/sc_man_scanner.h
-	COMMAND re2c --no-generation-date -s -o ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/sc_man_scanner.h ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/engine/sc_man_scanner.re
+	COMMAND ${ZDOOM_OUTPUT_DIR}/tools/re2c/re2c --no-generation-date -s -o ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/sc_man_scanner.h ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/engine/sc_man_scanner.re
 	DEPENDS re2c ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/engine/sc_man_scanner.re )
 
 include_directories( ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR} )
diff --git a/tools/lemon/CMakeLists.txt b/tools/lemon/CMakeLists.txt
index a4da050..488a5c7 100644
--- a/tools/lemon/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/tools/lemon/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 cmake_minimum_required( VERSION 3.16 )
 
-if( NOT CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
+if( NOT ZCMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
 	set( CMAKE_C_FLAGS_DEBUG "${CMAKE_C_FLAGS_DEBUG} -D_DEBUG" )
 
 	add_executable( lemon lemon.c )
diff --git a/tools/re2c/CMakeLists.txt b/tools/re2c/CMakeLists.txt
index 6b88877..449dc5e 100644
--- a/tools/re2c/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/tools/re2c/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 cmake_minimum_required( VERSION 3.16 )
 
-if( NOT CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
+if( NOT ZCMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
 
 include( CheckFunctionExists )
 include( CheckTypeSize )
diff --git a/tools/zipdir/CMakeLists.txt b/tools/zipdir/CMakeLists.txt
index d08ca09..de2fe20 100644
--- a/tools/zipdir/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/tools/zipdir/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 cmake_minimum_required( VERSION 3.16 )
 
-if( NOT CMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
+if( NOT ZCMAKE_CROSSCOMPILING )
 	include_directories( SYSTEM "${BZIP2_INCLUDE_DIR}" "${LZMA_INCLUDE_DIR}" )
 	add_executable( zipdir
 		zipdir.c )

```

### Replication

_No response_

### Configuration

```ini

```

### Log

_No response_

title: Fix sound engine fully flushing on full console
author: Boondorl
body: According to https://github.com/UZDoom/UZDoom/pull/1551#issuecomment-4895037824, when entering the full console mode (e.g. the console when in the main menu), the entire sound engine is restarted which includes re-parsing SNDINFOs. This can cause existing warnings to sound off again, making them too spammy. This should be fixed properly by only restarting the actual state of the sound engine, not everything related to it.

### Bug Description

After having used the Registry Editor to fix a system bug where all my cursors appear washed out (including the ones inside UZDoom), the cursor no longer appears in UZDoom's menus, regardless of what the in-game Mouse Settings are.

### Replication

Using the Registry Editor, Go to "Computer\HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Control Panel\Mouse", and set "MouseTrails" to "-1". Restart the PC to apply the change, then open UZDoom.

title: TakeBody() ZScript Function
author: kinker31
body:
### Game

All

### Problem Description

Attempting to work on a gameplay mod, and I wanna see about implementing inventory items that take away a fraction of the player's health when used. (e.g. powerup for a bit of player health, healing item that damages at first, etc.)

### Feature Description

I'd imagine TakeBody would work similar to GiveBody(), only that it subtracts the player's health (if the player can take damage) rather than add to it. For example, +15 would deal 15 damage to the player, and -40 would set the player's health to 40% of the max health if it was higher than that.

### Additional Media
